message:
fix(net): suppress spurious control traffic from cancel-ack and drain-request after shutdown

src/net/worker_channel.rs — Two code paths in WorkerCoordinator where
CancelAcknowledged and DrainRequest processing would emit new outbound
control traffic after shutdown_requested is set. Both paths now validate
the job ID exists (returning UnknownJobId error if not) and short-circuit
with Ok(()) when shutdown is in progress, avoiding spurious control
messages to a worker that is already shutting down. Without this fix,
late-arriving cancel acknowledgments or drain requests could trigger
follow-up messages that race with the shutdown sequence, potentially
causing the worker to see unexpected messages during teardown. Adds test
coordinator_shutdown_supersedes_cancel_acknowledged_follow_up verifying
the short-circuit behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
